title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience with deep hands-on work in AI search, ranking, personalization, and recommendation systems at Samsung.com and Amazon is a near-perfect domain match for Airbnb's R&P team. Key bullet points — hybrid AI search, search ranking via behavioral signals, recommendation API, and A/B experimentation frameworks — directly map to the role's core responsibilities. The only meaningful gap is the 10+ years requirement (Riley has ~9) and the lack of marketplace/two-sided platform experience (guests + hosts), which is central to Airbnb's context; however, Amazon's 2M+ B2B seller platform provides partial mitigation.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect archetype match (AI Search + Personalization + Recommendations) at a marquee consumer brand — one year short on YOE is a minor barrier worth pushing through.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving search relevance, personalization, and ML-powered discovery at global scale — shipping hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung and ML-driven listing intelligence for 2M+ sellers at Amazon. Passionate about building intelligent systems that connect users with exactly what they need, from query understanding to context-aware multi-turn discovery experiences.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: The 10+ years requirement is a soft barrier — Riley's 9 years are dense with exactly the right work (search ranking, personalization, ML systems at scale). Lead with Samsung's 10M+ MAU impact and Amazon's scale. For the two-sided marketplace gap, draw parallels between Amazon's seller/buyer duality and Airbnb's host/guest dynamic explicitly in your cover letter.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core competencies in AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and recommendation systems map almost perfectly to Airbnb's Guest Discovery & Homepage charter — the role is essentially "make it easy for guests to find the right listing," which mirrors Samsung.com's product discovery mandate at scale (10M+ monthly users). The requirement for AI/relevance model integration, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional leadership across design, engineering, data science, and marketing is well-covered by Riley's experience. The one notable gap is the explicit consumer marketplace / two-sided platform context at Airbnb's scale and the "product narrative / marketing strategy" responsibility, which leans toward a more senior communicator/leader profile than Riley's current PM III title — but at 9 years of experience, it's a competitive reach, not a disqualifier.
recommendation_reason: Domain, archetype, and AI discovery skills are a near-perfect match; 9 years of experience satisfies the 8+ year requirement, and Airbnb's SF/NY/LA locations align with Riley's preferred cities.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving search, discovery, and personalization experiences at scale — including hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-powered recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV platform. Proven track record shipping AI-powered discovery products that measurably improve engagement, conversion, and user satisfaction across consumer and marketplace contexts.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, launching an add-ons discovery page that boosted AOV by 15%; earlier work on a $2B+ GMV platform drove AOV from $148 to $185 (+25%).
gap_handling: The role asks for experience "creating product messaging and delivering to customers and the media" — Riley's resume is light on external narrative/GTM communications work. Counter this by briefly noting the AEO pipeline launch (8x AI agent traffic, +40% conversion in 12 weeks) as a cross-functional GTM execution win, and mention any product launch announcements, blog posts, or press coordination at Samsung if applicable. In the cover letter, explicitly call out comfort leading product narratives both internally (executive alignment at Samsung) and externally.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience — including deep e-commerce discovery, personalization/recommendation systems, A/B experimentation, and consumer-facing product launches at Samsung.com and Kolon — maps very well to this consumer shopping experience role. The preferred qualifications are nearly a checklist of Riley's background: e-commerce, search, personalization, and recommendation systems all appear prominently. The one meaningful gap is domain-specific: the role is specifically focused on Health/nutrition/dietary discovery, an area Riley has no direct experience in — but the JD signals this is a "nice to have" differentiator, not a hard requirement.
recommendation_reason: Strong core match on consumer e-commerce, discovery, personalization, and experimentation; Health domain gap is soft, not disqualifying.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building consumer discovery and personalization experiences at scale — including AI-powered search, recommendation systems, and guided shopping flows serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record shipping zero-to-one products with measurable impact (CTR +20–45%, AOV +25%, return rate -40%) through rigorous experimentation and deep cross-functional collaboration.
top_bullets: Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% (30% → 45%) and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
gap_handling: The Health/nutrition domain is the only notable gap. In your cover letter or interviews, bridge it by drawing parallels: dietary filters and health-forward discovery are fundamentally a personalization + filtering + content surface problem — exactly what you've solved in e-commerce. Emphasize your virtual try-on work (user-specific, behavior-driven recommendations) and guided shopping flows as analogues to health-guided discovery. If you have any personal connection to health/nutrition or wellness tech, briefly mention it.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, relevance ranking, query understanding, and experimentation is a strong match for Faire's search algorithms role — the core domain is nearly identical. The B2B wholesale marketplace context at Faire differs from Riley's B2C/DTC experience, but the algorithmic search ownership is directly transferable. The primary friction point is seniority: this is a Staff PM role requiring 7+ years in complex Search systems at a high-growth startup, and while Riley has 9 total years of experience, the deep search-specific tenure (Samsung, ~2 years) may fall short of "Staff" bar, and the role is a stretch above Riley's stated target of Senior/PM III. The in-office hybrid requirement in NYC or SF also creates a mild location tension from Bellevue.
recommendation_reason: Strong search algorithm alignment but Staff-level seniority bar and B2B wholesale context are meaningful stretches from Riley's Senior PM target and B2C background.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years driving AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale, including ownership of hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and relevance experimentation for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com. Proven track record partnering with data science and engineering to define metrics, run rigorous A/B tests, and ship search algorithm improvements that deliver measurable business outcomes.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff-level bar is the biggest risk. Riley should frame the Samsung role as owning the full search product end-to-end (vision, roadmap, algorithm strategy, metrics framework) — not just feature delivery — to signal Staff-caliber scope. Highlighting the 10M+ user scale, cross-org leadership, and CEO/exec alignment on global expansion helps close the seniority perception gap. Acknowledge B2B wholesale is a new domain but bridge it via the Amazon seller-tools experience and retailer-as-customer framing.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background and strong experimentation credentials (A/B testing, funnel optimization, CTR/conversion metrics) map well to the growth PM charter at Anthropic. The Samsung and Kolon e-commerce growth loops provide solid evidence of acquisition-to-monetization thinking. However, the role is explicitly developer/API platform growth — building for technical builders — and Riley's portfolio skews toward consumer-facing discovery and e-commerce, with no direct evidence of owning developer onboarding, API console, docs, billing, or self-serve upgrade flows for a B2B/API product. The Amazon seller tools role is the closest proxy but is B2B seller tooling, not an API/developer platform. The gap is real but bridgeable with strong positioning.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/growth fundamentals and brand-fit with Anthropic, but no direct developer platform or API product growth experience — resume needs a targeted reframe to compete.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth through AI-powered search, discovery, and intelligent product experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record owning full-funnel growth metrics — acquisition, activation, and monetization — with strong experimentation discipline and a deep passion for building at the frontier of AI technology.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — demonstrating self-serve, activation, and onboarding-funnel ownership closest to an API platform context.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, driving a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% through model optimization and iterative A/B testing — evidence of full-funnel growth thinking.
gap_handling: Directly address the developer platform gap in your cover letter: frame the Amazon seller tools work as owning a technical self-serve platform with onboarding, catalog pipelines, and B2B activation flows — the structural analog to an API console. Highlight any experience consuming or evaluating APIs (e.g., LLM integrations at Samsung), and emphasize your AEO pipeline work as evidence of building for technical/structured-data consumers. If you have any side projects or prototypes built on Claude or OpenAI APIs, lead with that.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML personalization background — spanning hybrid search, recommendation systems, LLM pipelines, and conversational AI — maps strongly to the technical demands of this role, and the Samsung.com and Kolon experiences provide direct evidence of shipping consumer-facing personalization at scale. However, this is a Staff PM role explicitly focused on a platform product (full-stack AI personalization infrastructure, RAG/retrieval architectures, multi-surface extensibility), which sits above Riley's target seniority range of Senior/PM III and tilts toward a platform archetype rather than Riley's core consumer/discovery archetype. The customer support domain (CS Platform) is also a meaningful domain shift — Riley has zero CS/support tooling experience, and the role's north star (emulating human agent judgment) is meaningfully different from e-commerce discovery or search relevance.
recommendation_reason: Strong technical personalization and AI signal fit, but the Staff-level seniority and CS platform domain are real gaps that make this competitive — apply only with heavy tailoring toward platform thinking and retrieval systems.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping personalization and AI-powered experiences at scale — from hybrid search ranking and recommendation systems on Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) to ML-driven personalization funnels on a $2B+ GMV platform. Experienced in LLM pipelines, contextual retrieval, and end-to-end experimentation strategy, with a track record of building foundational AI systems that power measurable outcomes across multiple surfaces.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup using LLM-driven pipelines, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The CS/support domain is unfamiliar territory — Riley should address this directly by drawing parallels between personalized discovery (helping users find the right product) and personalized support (helping users resolve the right issue), emphasizing that the underlying personalization stack, signals, and LLM architecture patterns are transferable.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths in AI-powered search, discovery, and personalization map partially to this role's AI-enabled self-service and discovery-led redesign mandate, and 9 years of PM experience clears the 5+ year bar comfortably. However, this role is fundamentally about customer support infrastructure, help center redesign, and CustOps stakeholder management — none of which appear meaningfully in Riley's background. The Growth-team context and conversion/retention framing are a reasonable domain bridge, but the absence of any support-enablement, knowledge-base, or help-center product experience is a real gap that will hurt competitively.
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/discovery/growth credentials partially fit, but no support-enablement or help-center experience is a meaningful gap for a role centered on CustOps tooling and Help Site redesign.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving discovery-led, AI-powered product experiences at scale — including conversational AI, personalized search, and self-service recommendation systems that measurably improve engagement and conversion across 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record leading cross-functional teams through complex platform redesigns, with deep experience using experimentation and behavioral data to optimize user journeys from trial to retention.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%, and designed segmentation-driven CRM program boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Proactively frame your conversational AI chat feature at Samsung as a self-service deflection surface — it functionally reduced user friction the same way a Help Center does. If you apply, add a cover note explicitly connecting your AI discovery work to support deflection metrics and reference any experience with chatbot flows, FAQ surfacing, or zero-result-state handling in search as analogues to knowledge-base product thinking.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, strong experimentation chops, and SQL/Tableau/data visualization skills map well onto Lyft's core requirements — particularly the KPI tracking, cross-functional alignment, and ML-driven product delivery. However, this role is squarely focused on an internal AI Platform (agentic/multi-agent orchestration, platform evangelism, build-vs-buy decisions) rather than consumer-facing search or discovery, which is Riley's primary archetype. The explicit ask for agentic systems expertise, multi-agent orchestration, and hands-on coding familiarity (PyTorch, Keras, Scikit-learn) represent real gaps. Additionally, "Staff PM" at Lyft is a meaningful stretch above Riley's target seniority, and the SF hybrid requirement (3 days/week) from Bellevue adds friction.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and data credentials partially fit, but the platform/agentic AI focus, Staff-level stretch, and SF in-office requirement are meaningful hurdles that reduce overall conviction.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping ML-driven platforms — from hybrid search and LLM pipelines to personalization APIs and conversational AI — at scale across Samsung, Amazon, and global e-commerce. Proven track record defining KPIs, leading cross-functional technical teams, and evangelizing AI capabilities to executive stakeholders to drive measurable adoption and business impact.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley should directly address the agentic/multi-agent gap in a cover letter by framing the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as adjacent experience with orchestrated AI workflows, while acknowledging a learning curve on pure platform PM responsibilities. Highlighting the LLM product evaluation and multi-agent workflow skills listed under "Familiar" as active areas of development (with concrete examples if possible) would strengthen the application.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI product and e-commerce fundamentals — search, discovery, personalization, A/B testing, and cross-functional delivery are all well-evidenced. However, this role is explicitly a Monetization/Growth PM role requiring deep ownership of subscription pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion funnels, and consumption-based billing — areas Riley has touched (AOV optimization, onboarding conversion, CRM retention at Kolon) but has never owned as a primary charter. The Samsung and Amazon roles are search/discovery and B2B platform roles, not growth or monetization roles, which creates a meaningful archetype mismatch for a role where "majority in growth focused roles" is a stated requirement.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and e-commerce credentials but limited direct monetization/subscription growth ownership creates a real gap for a role that requires it as core expertise.
suggested_summary: Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered product experiences at Samsung and Amazon, with a track record of growth-oriented outcomes including conversion rate improvements, AOV increases, and retention gains across platforms serving 10M+ users. Passionate about building at the frontier of AI and experienced in data-driven experimentation, user funnel optimization, and cross-functional execution — eager to apply these skills to monetization strategy at Anthropic.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The core gap is direct monetization PM ownership (pricing/packaging, free-to-paid funnels, subscription billing). Riley should address this head-on in the cover letter by reframing Kolon growth work — first purchase rate +30%, retention +7%, AOV +25% — as funnel monetization outcomes, and acknowledge excitement about expanding into pure monetization strategy. Avoid claiming deep subscription billing experience that isn't there.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, SQL skills, and experience building LLM-powered and data-intensive products (AEO pipelines, conversational AI, recommendation systems) are genuine and relevant — but the role centers on B2B SaaS, business intelligence, semantic layers, and data warehouse tooling (the "modern data stack"), which is a meaningful domain gap. Riley's background is almost entirely e-commerce/consumer-facing, not enterprise analytics or BI, and the role explicitly requires domain knowledge of BI tools, data warehouses, and governance — areas with little evidence on the resume. The 0-to-1, NYC-hybrid, enterprise-facing nature also diverges from Riley's consumer/e-commerce archetype.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credentials and SQL skills are a real foundation, but the BI/data stack domain gap and NYC hybrid location requirement are meaningful friction points that require deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-powered search, conversational AI, and data-driven product experiences at Samsung and Amazon, with a track record of shipping 0-to-1 AI systems that drive measurable business outcomes at scale. Passionate about making AI-powered analytics trustworthy and governed, with hands-on experience in prompt engineering, ML pipeline design, and SQL-driven decision-making across complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — supporting product discovery and comparison for 10M+ monthly users
Defined and delivered an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic 8x and increasing conversion by 40% in 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system leveraging multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The core gap is BI/data stack domain knowledge — Riley has no evident experience with tools like dbt, Looker, Snowflake, or semantic layer concepts. To bridge this, Riley should explicitly reference the AEO metadata pipeline and CDP work (BigQuery, Tableau, GA) as hands-on data infrastructure experience, and consider adding a short cover note that connects the "context layer" concept to her search relevance and instrumentation work. Studying the modern data stack (dbt, Metabase, Databricks, warehouse-native BI) before interviewing is essential.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and recommendation systems at Samsung and Amazon — that signal genuine depth in AI product work. However, the Claude Code role is specifically developer tooling (CLI, agentic coding workflows), and Riley's background is firmly in e-commerce discovery and consumer AI, not dev tools. The hard requirement of "at least 1 year as a professional engineer" is also a potential disqualifier — Riley's resume shows no engineering tenure, which is a meaningful gap for this role.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credentials and Anthropic sponsors visas, but the dev tools domain and "1 year as an engineer" requirement are real gaps that make this competitive.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from hybrid search and conversational AI to agentic recommendation pipelines — at Samsung and Amazon, serving 10M+ users and 2M+ sellers. Passionate about translating frontier model capabilities into high-leverage product experiences, with a hands-on approach to experimentation, cross-functional execution, and AI tooling that accelerates how people work.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The "1 year as a professional engineer" requirement is the hardest gap to address. If Riley has any engineering background (coursework, side projects, early-career coding work, or contributions to technical pipelines), it should be surfaced explicitly on the resume — even framed as "technical co-builder" roles. Additionally, the application should acknowledge deep hands-on engagement with Claude Code and AI coding tools (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) to compensate for the lack of formal dev tools PM experience.

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and recommendation systems — which map well to Okta's AI-native product requirements. However, the role is "Principal PM" (10+ years required, enterprise B2B internal tooling focus), which is a meaningful seniority stretch above Riley's target Senior/PM III level, and Riley's domain experience is squarely in e-commerce/search rather than identity, enterprise SaaS, or internal IT/HR/Finance workflows. The "Customer Zero" framing (building internal AI products that inform Okta's market offerings) also skews toward enterprise platform/infra PM archetype rather than Riley's consumer/discovery specialization.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML shipping credentials are a real asset, but the Principal-level seniority, enterprise identity domain, and internal-tooling focus are meaningful gaps that make this a stretch application worth attempting only with heavy tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping production AI/ML systems — including LLM-driven pipelines, conversational AI, and agentic automation — across platforms serving 10M+ users. Proven track record taking AI products from 0→1 through broad adoption, with deep experience building AI evaluation frameworks, defining quality metrics, and leading cross-functional teams to deliver measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Address the enterprise/identity domain gap head-on in your cover letter by framing your "Customer Zero" parallel: at Samsung you were effectively Customer Zero for Samsung.com's AI search stack, building internal-facing AI infrastructure (AEO pipelines, ranking systems) that informed external product direction. Acknowledge the step up to Principal level by emphasizing scope (10M+ users, global multi-market launches) and cross-functional leadership breadth across engineering, data science, UX, and marketing.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. Passionate about the next frontier of AI agent identity and governance, bringing a deep track record of cross-functional technical collaboration, data-driven experimentation, and zero-to-one product delivery.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is IAM domain knowledge (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust). Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core PM competencies — roadmapping, cross-functional execution, data-driven experimentation, and AI curiosity — are genuinely strong and transferable. However, the role is squarely centered on enterprise B2B work management (task/project UX, complex data models, enterprise compliance), which is a significant domain departure from Riley's AI search, e-commerce discovery, and consumer personalization background. Riley's Amazon B2B experience is the closest touch point, but it was a seller-tools platform role rather than end-user productivity/collaboration software. There is little evidence of UX-heavy systems thinking for complex task/project data models, enterprise-grade feature scoping, or SaaS collaboration product work.
recommendation_reason: Strong PM fundamentals and AI curiosity meet the bar, but domain (enterprise work management/SaaS UX) and product archetype (B2B platform UX) are a meaningful stretch from Riley's e-commerce/AI search core; requires deliberate tailoring to compete.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered products at scale, including enterprise B2B tooling at Amazon serving 2M+ sellers and platform experiences used by 10M+ monthly users. Proven systems thinker who bridges complex data modeling, cross-functional execution, and human-centric UX to ship high-velocity roadmaps with measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories (Amazon)

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI; led phased rollout with A/B testing, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers; identified key issues via 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys, partnering with 5 dependent teams to deliver a scalable solution (Amazon)
gap_handling: The biggest gap is lack of direct SaaS/work-management product experience — Asana's interviewers will probe on this. Bridge it by framing the Amazon role as enterprise B2B platform work at massive scale, and emphasize systems-thinking examples (e.g., data model design, complex UX flows, multi-team dependency management) from both Samsung and Amazon. Be ready to articulate genuine curiosity about the task/project collaboration space and show you've used Asana or competitor products deeply.

title: Senior Product Manager, APM - Observability
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths are in AI-powered search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — domains that are meaningfully different from the APM/Observability/distributed tracing focus this role demands. While Elastic is a "Search AI Company" and Riley has strong search credentials, this role is squarely in DevOps/developer tooling (APM, Synthetics, RUM, OpenTelemetry) — a vertical with no evidence in Riley's resume. The seniority level and remote-US setup are good fits, but the domain gap is the dominant factor pulling the score down.
recommendation_reason: Hard domain mismatch — APM, distributed tracing, and observability tooling are not represented anywhere in Riley's background, making this a stretch that would require significant re-skilling narrative.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered data pipelines, structured metadata systems, and instrumentation strategies at scale — including behavioral signal instrumentation and search telemetry at Samsung.com for 10M+ monthly users. Bringing a customer-backwards product methodology and cross-functional execution track record to complex, data-rich developer-facing product domains.
top_bullets: Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, leveraging behavioral insights to iterate search experiences — increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: If Riley wants to pursue this role, the cover letter must directly acknowledge the domain shift and frame transferable signals: instrumentation strategy, telemetry pipeline ownership, and structured data work at Samsung/Amazon are the closest bridges. Riley would also benefit from demonstrating any hands-on familiarity with OpenTelemetry, Elastic Stack, or developer tooling in a side project or self-study — without that, the application will likely be screened out early by recruiters filtering for APM experience.

title: Senior Group Product Manager, Advertiser Optimization (Ads Marketplace)
score_rationale: This role is a Senior Group Product Manager — a people manager leading multiple PMs across Reddit's Ads Marketplace (bidding, targeting, budget optimization), which is a domain Riley has zero direct experience in. Riley's background is deeply rooted in AI search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — not ad tech, auction mechanics, advertiser-facing tooling, or ads optimization. Additionally, Riley's target seniority explicitly avoids Group/Director-level roles, and this title requires proven people management of PM teams, which is not evidenced anywhere on the resume. The domain and level mismatch are both fundamental.
recommendation_reason: Double mismatch: ad tech domain is entirely absent from Riley's background, and the Group PM people-management scope exceeds stated target seniority and demonstrated experience.
suggested_summary: Experienced Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered discovery and personalization products at scale — not a fit for this Ads Marketplace Group PM role due to lack of ad tech domain experience and people management scope. Recommend targeting Senior IC PM roles in search, discovery, or consumer AI instead.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%.
Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, boosting AOV by 15%.
gap_handling: The two core gaps here are structural and hard to paper over: (1) Zero ad tech / ads marketplace experience — Riley has never worked on bidding systems, auction mechanics, advertiser ROI tools, or ad targeting. Reddit will screen for this specifically. (2) No people management track record — this role requires hiring and developing a team of PMs, which is not evidenced on the resume. These are not resume-framing problems; they are genuine experience gaps that cannot be bridged by tailoring alone.
